column2: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation. It can be concrete, abstract, utility, nested, an exception class, a legacy class, or a wrapper class.

A method is a block of code that performs a specific task, defined within a class or interface, and can be invoked on objects or directly via the class (if static). It includes instance methods, static methods, and constructors.
An interface defines a contract of methods that a class must implement. It can be functional, annotation-based, or nested.
A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces for better organization.
